revision.comment: Programmatically generating so that if this gets imported to wikipedia as a draft, the bot will attribute it to Immanuelle and send them g6 warnings

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Martial temple]] and [[Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide?utm_source=chatgpt.com For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez?utm_source=chatgpt.com == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Martial temple]] and [[Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide?utm_source=chatgpt.com For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez?utm_source=chatgpt.com == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[Category:dd]][[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide?utm_source=chatgpt.com For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez?utm_source=chatgpt.com == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:kk]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:kk]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[en:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]]}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:1 revision imported]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[Category:dd]][[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide?utm_source=chatgpt.com For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez?utm_source=chatgpt.com == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[en:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]]}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide?utm_source=chatgpt.com For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez?utm_source=chatgpt.com == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{Gokoku shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[en:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]]}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide?utm_source=chatgpt.com For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez?utm_source=chatgpt.com == See Also == * [[Controversies The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide?utm_source=chatgpt.com For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez?utm_source=chatgpt.com == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[Category:dd]][[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide?utm_source=chatgpt.com For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez?utm_source=chatgpt.com == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[Category:dd]][[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide?utm_source=chatgpt.com For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez?utm_source=chatgpt.com == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tottori Gokoku Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]]{{Gokoku shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tottori Gokoku Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tottori Gokoku Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]]}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[Category:dd]][[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide?utm_source=chatgpt.com For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez?utm_source=chatgpt.com == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[Category:dd]][[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide?utm_source=chatgpt.com For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez?utm_source=chatgpt.com == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide?utm_source=chatgpt.com For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez?utm_source=chatgpt.com == See Also == * [[Controversies revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[Category:dd]][[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Gokoku {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[Category:dd]][[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Gokoku The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[Category:qq]][[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]]{{Gokoku shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[Category:Tottori Gokoku Gokoku Shrine]][[en:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]]}} \[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[Category:11]][[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]]{{Gokoku shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[Category:11]][[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Gokoku {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[Category:11]][[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] [[sn:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tottori Gokoku Shrine}}

The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. {{draft}} {{Promising draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tottori Gokoku Shrine|simple||ja|鳥取県護国神社}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} \ {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tottori Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] [[sn:ottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]]

It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} \ {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tottori Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] [[sn:ottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ja import failed]]

The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan.
