PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[sn:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] [[sn:ottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn: failed]]

It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[sn:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis but with jawiki links]][[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] [[sn:ottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]]

(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tottori Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|鳥取県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tottori Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|鳥取県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[sn:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] [[sn:ottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[sn:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[Category:pages with link uncertainty]]

Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[sn:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] [[sn:ottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[sn:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[Category:pages with link uncertainty]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:31, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tottori Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|鳥取県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[sn:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] [[sn:ottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[sn:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[Category:pages with link uncertainty]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. (Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tottori Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|鳥取県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[sn:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] [[sn:ottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[sn:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[Category:pages with link uncertainty]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tottori Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{coordinates|N|35.53189444185323|E|134.2283492756464}} {{coord|35.53189444185323|N|134.2283492756464|E}} [[Category:Ja import failed]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:31, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35.53189444185323|N|134.2283492756464|E}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tottori Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q135476768|simple|ja|鳥取県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q135476768|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[Category:pages with link uncertainty]] {{coordinates|N|35.53189444185323|E|134.2283492756464}} {{coord|35.53189444185323|N|134.2283492756464|E}} [[Category:Ja import failed]] {{wikidata link|Q135476768}}

(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tottori Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q135476768|simple|ja|鳥取県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q135476768|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] {{coordinates|N|35.53189444185323|E|134.2283492756464}} {{coord|35.53189444185323|N|134.2283492756464|E}} {{wikidata link|Q135476768}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:31, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35.53189444185323|N|134.2283492756464|E}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tottori Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q135476768|simple|ja|鳥取県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q135476768|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] {{coordinates|N|35.53189444185323|E|134.2283492756464}} {{coord|35.53189444185323|N|134.2283492756464|E}} {{wikidata link|Q135476768}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q135476768}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tottori Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q135476768|simple|ja|鳥取県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q135476768|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] {{coordinates|N|35.53189444185323|E|134.2283492756464}} {{coord|35.53189444185323|N|134.2283492756464|E}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q135476768}}

(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tottori Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q135476768|simple|ja|鳥取県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q135476768|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (鳥取県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Tottori City, Tottori Prefecture, Japan. Gokoku shrines, known as "護国神社" (Gokoku Jinja) in Japanese, are dedicated to honoring the spirits of those who died in wars, serving as places of reverence and reflection.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near the famous Tottori Sand Dunes, the shrine offers visitors a serene environment complemented by traditional Shinto architecture. The proximity to the sand dunes provides a unique backdrop, enhancing the shrine's tranquil atmosphere. https://www.thatch.co/guide/3zsvpgwt1ywz/view Visitors to the shrine can explore its peaceful grounds, which are surrounded by lush greenery, making it an ideal spot for reflection or a quiet stroll. The beautiful torii gates and tranquil paths offer picturesque settings for photography enthusiasts. https://wanderlog.com/view/ovsavlnoml/tottoris-anime-towns--sand-dunes-guide For those interested in Japanese history and culture, Tottori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine provides insight into the nation's efforts to honor and remember those who have served. Its location near other attractions, such as the Tottori Sand Dunes, makes it a worthwhile addition to any travel itinerary in the region. The shrine is located at Hamasaka, 1318-53, Tottori, Tottori 680-0001, Japan. For more information, visitors can contact the shrine at +81 857-22-4428. https://zaubee.com/biz/tottori-prefecture-gokoku-shrine-1mav63ez == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[ja:鳥取県護国神社]] {{coordinates|N|35.53189444185323|E|134.2283492756464}} {{coord|35.53189444185323|N|134.2283492756464|E}} {{wikidata link|Q135476768}} [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
